documents.title: Aiming Patterns

documents.title: Analog Output
documents.summary: The system has an analog output on the External Light connector capable of generating between 0 V and 10 V.

documents.title: Decode Leds
documents.summary: The device has a 360° LED decode indicator that flashes green upon successful decode and red upon job failure.

documents.title: Digital Industrial GPIO
documents.summary: Unlike optocoupled GPIO, digital GPIO actively drive the output signal high and low for significantly faster turn-on and turn-off time. Digital GPIO is not isolated and references the power supply and ground of the system. COMMON_IN and COMMON_OUT do not need to be terminated to use digital GPIO.

documents.title: General Purpose Input and Outputs
documents.summary: The device has two types of general-purpose inputs and outputs (GPIO).

documents.title: Optically Coupled GPIO
documents.summary: Optocoupled GPIO are electrically isolated from the rest of the system and require external reference through the COMMON_IN and COMMON_OUT wires.

documents.title: Power and Thermal Management
documents.summary: Algorithms keep the system within acceptable power and thermal parameters to ensure reliable operation over the device's lifetime.

documents.title: Supported Display Resolutions
documents.summary: Display resolution is automatically negotiated upon connection. Displays with at least 1920 x 1080 resolution provide the preferred user experience.

documents.title: User Interface Label
documents.summary: The User Interface (UI) label uses LEDs to provide information on device state and feedback. Two sensor PCB switches\_control the device's trigger and tune buttons. The TRIG switch acts as a trigger, and the TUNE switch adjusts and optimizes focus.

documents.title: Debugging
documents.summary: After you have mounted the camera, you can use basic script debugging features. The device always sends script execution data when in deploy mode.

documents.title: Using the Javascript Environment
documents.summary: JavaScript formatting provides the capability of post-processing inspection results, changing job status or FTP path, and sending manipulated results to differe

documents.title: Example Script Lifecycle
documents.summary: The lifecycle of the script includes four stages: deployment, two iterations, and edit mode.

documents.title: Program Structure
documents.summary: Every script must contain the function `onResults`. This function takes one argument as a data structure and returns the structure.

documents.title: Text Encoding
documents.summary: The read content of the barcode, data code, or other tools offering output as text is treated as a single-byte encoding in accordance with ISO-8859-1.

documents.title: Binarize
documents.summary: The Binarize Tool converts the image to monochrome.

documents.title: Blob Count
documents.summary: The Blob Count tool counts the number of blobs that pass certain filter parameters within a Region of Interest (ROI).

documents.title: Blob Presence Absence
documents.summary: The Blob Presence/Absence tool verifies a blob's presence or absence within a Region of Interest (ROI).

documents.title: Brightness
documents.summary: The Brightness tool calculates the average pixel brightness value in a Region of Interest (ROI).

documents.title: Circle Diameter
documents.summary: The Circle Diameter tool measures the diameter of a circle located in the Region of Interest (ROI).

documents.title: Close
documents.summary: The Close tool increases the image's overall brightness and enhances the remaining dark features by contrast.

documents.title: Common Machine Vision Tool Settings
documents.summary: Some machine vision tools share common settings. Refer to the table below to understand how to configure them based on your use case.

documents.title: Contrast
documents.summary: The Contrast tool calculates the maximum and minimum pixel intensity difference in a Region of Interest (ROI).

documents.title: Counting Tools
documents.summary: Counting tools determine the instances of particular objects within the Region of Interest (ROI).

documents.title: Datacode
documents.summary: Configure Datacode settings such as No Read String, Module Size, or Search Strategy.

documents.title: Deploying Machine Vision Jobs
documents.summary: To build and deploy a vision scanning (VS) job, start by selecting a machine vision tool and dragging it onto the FlowBuilder.

documents.title: Dilate
documents.summary: The Dilate tool replaces each pixel with a maximum of pixels within the kernel. This tool also thickens bright features in an image and reduces dark features.

documents.title: Distance
documents.summary: The Distance tool determines the space between two specifically defined objects or patterns in an image.

documents.title: Edge Count
documents.summary: The Edge Count tool identifies transitions based on the highest contrast level in a Region of Interest (ROI).

documents.title: Edge Detect
documents.summary: The Edge Detect tool identifies transitions based on the highest contrast in a Region of Interest (ROI).

documents.title: Edges
documents.summary: The Edge tool compares edges given input image with an image stored in a model created in the Training tab.

documents.title: Erode
documents.summary: The operation replaces each pixel with the darkest pixel in its neighborhood, thus shrinking bright areas and expanding the dark ones.

documents.title: Filter Tools
documents.summary: Filter Tools are facilitated by the use of a kernel. A kernel is repeatedly centered at each pixel within the dimensions of the region that is being...

documents.title: Flaw Detection
documents.summary: Flaw Detection tools perform a pixel-to-pixel comparison of two images. This is helpful for cases where the object's surface or shape is complex.

documents.title: Locate Object Count
documents.summary: Use the Locate Object Count tool to find a pattern in the image and set a match threshold for a candidate to be considered a match.

documents.title: Gradient Full
documents.summary: The Gradient Full tool brightens horizontal and vertical edges within the defined area of interest.

documents.title: Gradient Vertical
documents.summary: The Gradient Vertical tool brightens vertical edges within the defined region of interest.

documents.title: Identification Tools
documents.summary: Use the Identification Tools to read barcodes, DPM, Datacode, and Deep Learning OCR.

documents.title: Intensity
documents.summary: The intensity tool detects edges given input image with an image stored in a model created in the Training tab and then, as a result, creates a region...

documents.title: Locate Blob
documents.summary: A blob is a set of connected light-or dark-colored pixels. The Locate Blob tool detects blobs that pass specific filter parameters within a region of interest.

documents.title: Locate Circle
documents.summary: Locate Circle finds a circle model inside a user-defined region of interest that fits the specified parameters.

documents.title: Locate Edge
documents.summary: Locate Edge identifies transitions based on the highest contract in a user-defined region of interest.

documents.title: Locate Object Plus
documents.summary: Locate Object Plus finds specific patterns in a specified region of interest and evaluates them based on advanced characteristics such as minimum edge contrast and scale factor.

documents.title: Locate Object
documents.summary: Locate Object finds a specific pattern in a filmstrip based on an edge inside a user-defined region of interest.

documents.title: Locate Tools
documents.summary: Locate tools find a single occurrence of a predefined template on an image by comparing object edges.

documents.title: Measurement Tools
documents.summary: Use measurement tools to measure the distance between two objects, the width of an object, or the diameter of a circle in the region of interest.

documents.title: Measure Object Width
documents.summary: The Measure Object Width tool measures the width of an object present in an image.

documents.title: Object Presence Absence
documents.summary: The Object Presence Absence tool verifies the presence or absence of a specific pattern or object within a Region of Interest (ROI).

documents.title: Object Plus Presence Absence
documents.summary: The Object Plus Presence Absence tool finds specific patterns in a Region of Interest (ROI) and evaluates them based on advanced characteristics such as...

documents.title: Open
documents.summary: The Open tool decreases the image's overall brightness and enhances the remaining bright features by contrast.

documents.title: Pixel Count
documents.summary: The Pixel Count tool provides the number of pixels of a user-specified value (0-255) within a Region of Interest (ROI).

documents.title: Presence/absence Tools
documents.summary: Use Presence/Absence tools to detect specific objects or patterns within a Region of Interest (ROI).

documents.title: Read Barcode
documents.summary: Configure barcode decode settings such as Inverse 1D, ROI overlap, or ROI search.

documents.title: Read Dpm and Barcode
documents.summary: Configure DPM and barcode decode settings such as Inverse 1D, a no-read string, or barcode string match.

documents.title: Read Dpm
documents.summary: Configure DPM decode settings such as Inverse 1D, barcode string match, or ROI search.

documents.title: Using Machine Vision Tools
documents.summary: Machine Vision tools in Aurora Focus enable automated optical inspection by combining multiple tool types into a single job workflow using the FlowBuilder.

documents.title: Gradient Horizontal
documents.summary: The Gradient Horizontal tool brightens horizontal edges within the defined region of interest.

documents.title: Using Anomaly Detection
documents.summary: Anomaly Detection is available on FS42 and NS42 devices only.

documents.title: Using Accelerated Tools
documents.summary: Accelerate Deep Learning OCR and Anomaly Detection tools on FS42 and NS42 devices for faster inspection speeds.
